mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k signals strong labor market demand, boosting corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 70% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k growth.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening economic demand, creating tail risks that depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in 60% of periods where both INDPRO and HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60% of instances where CPI exceeds 2.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicates deteriorating supply chain efficiency, creating negative momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 70% of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12mo MA.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real sales (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) signals collapsing consumer and business demand, creating a negative feedback loop that drives S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in 50% of periods where both RSAFS and GDPC1 decline.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 5% indicates weakening labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending confidence and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 6-month high signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 70% of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 6mo high.
